rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS SCHRIER VS. DEAN NICOLACAKIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition And Petition To Confirm Supplement To Final Contractual Arbitration Award And For Petition Related Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 30, 2021 line 1. CLAIMANTS DEAN NICOLACAKIS AND MARIA NICOLACAKIS' NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SUPPLEMENT TO FINAL CONTRACTUAL ARBITRATION AWARD AND FOR PETITION RELATED FEES AND COSTS is GRANTED as requested. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: FAST CAPITAL, LLC. ET AL VS. LA COSTA LOANS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 30, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANT CHONG HONG'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts (not conclusions) in support of each cause of action against the moving defendant. Complaint shall be alleged in clear and concise language. Amended Complaint shall be filed and served within 10 days of notice of entry of order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: FAST CAPITAL, LLC. ET AL VS. LA COSTA LOANS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Defaults Of La Costa Loans, Inc., Richard Shrigley, And Sf San Bruno, Llc
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 30, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANTS LA COSTA LOANS, INC., RICHARD SHRIGLEY AND SF SAN BRUNO, LLC'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULTS is GRANTED. Defendant to file a response in 15 days of notice of entry of order. Defendant shall respond to the operative complaint if Plaintiff does not file an amended complaint (see ruling on co-defendant's demurrer). If an amended complaint is filed and served, Defendant to respond to the amended complaint per code. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
